title: Calorhabdos axillaris
intro:

Calorhabdos calycinum, commonly known as the Goldie or Goldie's fan petiole, is a small plant species belonging to the family Crassulaceae. It is a succulent herb with medicinal properties, particularly known for its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Calorhabdos calycinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calorhabdos calycinum is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to possess antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects.
